meta.document: COPS Program Grant Structure — DFR Relevance

dfr_relevant_grant_types.name: COPS Technology and Equipment Program
dfr_relevant_grant_types.purpose: Fund technology and equipment that enhances law enforcement capabilities

real_world_examples.use: Drones, docking systems, software and equipment for DFR program
Full DFR program: law enforcement, fire, traffic reconstruction, weather monitoring

dfr_relevant_grant_types.match_requirement: Varies — typically 25% local match, some programs offer full federal funding

dfr_relevant_grant_types.name: COPS Hiring Program
dfr_relevant_grant_types.purpose: Fund officer positions — can include drone pilots
dfr_relevant_grant_types.dfr_eligible: indirect — can fund remote pilot FTE positions
dfr_relevant_grant_types.notes: Use for staffing DFR program operators, not equipment

who_can_apply: Local law enforcement agencies (cities, counties, townships)
who_cannot_apply: Non-law enforcement municipal agencies (fire, EMS apply separately)
eligibility.ndaa_compliance_notes: Must certify that UAS equipment does not include covered components under NDAA §848. Blue UAS-listed platforms are the lowest-friction path.
eligibility.asda_compliance_notes: American Security Drone Act applies to DOJ grant recipients. Prohibits use of funds to procure UAS from covered entities.

narrative_key_points: Quantify response time improvement expected (cite local incident data)
Reference crime types where DFR provides documented advantage (active shooter, pursuit, missing persons)
Show cost savings vs. helicopter deployment ($500–$2,000/hour helicopter vs. <$50/hour drone)
Include officer safety benefits — drone provides situational awareness before officers arrive
Reference peer agency deployments (Chula Vista PD, Scottsdale PD as proven models)
common_mistakes: Proposing DJI or Autel equipment (covered entities — grant will be denied or clawed back)
Failing to include NDAA compliance certification language
Underestimating FAA waiver timeline in project schedule
Not including community engagement / transparency plan (increasingly required)

timeline.reimbursement_note: COPS grants are typically reimbursement-based — agency pays upfront, submits for reimbursement
